serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Glazed Mochimochi is one donut, weighing approximately 60 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Health Promotion Board Singapore — Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values adapted from 'Doughnut, glazed' and 'Mochi' entries, with adjustments for mochi-like texture and glaze, as a direct entry for 'Glazed Mochimochi' was not found. Confidence is medium due to adaptation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values for 100 Foods
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI estimated based on 'Doughnut, glazed' (GI 76) and 'Mochi' (GI 70-80 depending on preparation). Given the high sugar content and deep-fried nature, a GI of 75 is a reasonable estimate for this ultra-processed food.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar, high saturated fat, and deep-fried preparation.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid Glazed Mochimochi due to its high sugar (30g/100g) and saturated fat (10g/100g) content, which can contribute to insulin-mediated sodium retention and increased cardiovascular risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Extremely high sugar and refined carbohydrates with low fiber.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Glazed Mochimochi is highly unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very high sugar (30g/100g) and refined carbohydrate (50g/100g with only 0.83g/100g fiber) content, leading to rapid blood glucose spikes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, trans fat, sugar, and deep-fried preparation.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This food should be avoided by individuals with cardiovascular disease due to its high saturated fat (10g/100g), trans fat (0.33g/100g), and sugar content, all of which negatively impact heart health.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates AGEs, high sugar and saturated fat promote inflammation.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Glazed Mochimochi as deep-frying creates advanced glycation end products (AGEs) that exacerbate inflammation, compounded by high sugar and saturated fat.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Glazed Mochimochi is a popular fried snack, often categorized as a donut, known for its distinctive chewy, mochi-like texture. It is typically deep-fried and then coated in a sweet glaze, offering a delightful contrast between its soft, elastic interior and sugary exterior. Originating from Singapore, this dessert is a fusion of traditional mochi and Western donut concepts. Nutritionally, it is very high in energy (416.67 kcal/100g), carbohydrates (50g/100g), and particularly sugar (30g/100g), with a significant amount of fat (21.67g/100g), including saturated fat (10g/100g). Its low fiber content (0.83g/100g) and high glycemic index (estimated at 75) make it a food that can cause rapid blood glucose increases. Due to its nutritional profile and preparation method, it is generally considered unsuitable for individuals managing conditions like Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, and Cardiovascular Disease.

confidence_reason: Nutrient data is adapted from similar items (glazed donut, mochi) from HPB Singapore Foodo, as an exact match for 'Glazed Mochimochi' was not available. GI is an analogous estimate. The overall profile is consistent with deep-fried, glazed, high-sugar products.
notes: Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, vitamin A, B vitamins, folate, vitamin D, E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water values are set to -1 as detailed data for this specific adapted food item is not available in the primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo).
